leads.issues: Missing meta description across all pages, reducing organic click-through rate. Multi-step consultation process (questionnaire → phone call → consultation) creating significant drop-off friction. Service/pricing descriptions are vague with no package comparison or pricing context on homepage. Trust signals (credentials, client results) buried below the fold — not visible on first impression.
leads.opportunity: Streamline consultation flow to a single Calendly booking step above the fold, add meta descriptions and LocalBusiness schema markup, and move credentials and social proof above the fold. Outcome: higher organic CTR from Google, reduced enquiry friction, and more direct bookings from first-time visitors.
leads.email_subject: Quick question about your website, Sammy
leads.email_body:
Hi Sammy,

I was looking at SammyShreds.com.au and noticed your site doesn't have a meta description set anywhere. In Google search results, that means random text appears instead of a compelling reason to click, which quietly costs you visits every week.

I put together a quick page showing what I'd improve and what it could mean for your bookings:
{{FUNNEL_LINK}}

If any of it resonates, I'd love to chat. You can also see how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure either way.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: No meta description on any page, limiting click-through rates from Google. Duplicate navigation menu appearing twice in the HTML, creating crawl inefficiency. Contact form buried mid-page behind six content sections. Xero Gold Partner badge and awards are trust signals hidden at the bottom of the page rather than above the fold where they influence the decision.
leads.opportunity: Fix technical SEO foundation (duplicate nav, meta descriptions, LocalBusiness schema), move trust signals and a specific CTA above the fold, and reposition the contact form higher on key pages. Outcome: improved local search rankings for Brisbane bookkeeping terms, higher CTR, and more direct form submissions.

leads.issues: No meta description on any page. No contact form on the homepage, forcing visitors to navigate away before they can enquire. No LocalBusiness schema markup, making the site ineligible for Google Maps local pack and reducing local search visibility. A visible typo on the homepage ('enegery efficient') undermines credibility. Weak CTA hierarchy with competing navigation links rather than a clear primary action.
leads.opportunity: Add LocalBusiness schema and meta descriptions to unlock local search and Maps visibility, embed a quote request form directly on the homepage, and fix the CTA hierarchy to funnel visitors toward one clear action. Outcome: eligibility for Perth local 3-pack results, higher homepage lead capture rate, and a more trustworthy first impression.
leads.email_subject: Quick note about your site — Electrical Company WA
leads.email_body:
